title: crotalaria eremicola subsp. eremicola
intro: Crotalaria eremicola subsp. eremicola, also known as the Desert Rattle or Eremicola Rattlepod, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional African medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant is said to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and alleviating digestive problems, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. In herbal preparations, the leaves and seeds of Crotalaria eremicola subsp. eremicola are often used to make teas, infusions, or decoctions, which are believed to harness the plant's medicinal properties.
